questions: Tell me a scary urban legend
Most famous urban legends in the world
Why do people believe urban legends?
answers: Urban legends are modern folk stories that spread through word of mouth or the internet, often presented as true events. They usually involve horror, humor, or cautionary themes and reflect societal fears.
Famous urban legends include Bloody Mary, the Hook Man, alligators in sewers, and the Vanishing Hitchhiker. These stories persist because they tap into common fears and are easy to retell.

Urban legends holo adhunik golpo jegulo shotti ghotona hishabe choray kintu usually fictional. Internet er jughe egulo social media te khub taratari spread hoy. Egulo somaj er bhoy ar anxiety reflect kore.

People believe urban legends because they often contain a grain of plausibility and play on real fears. They spread quickly because they're entertaining and serve as modern cautionary tales about dangers in everyday life.
